predicates.definition: Confirm a text/alternative for the time-based media exists and conveys information equivalent to the original audio or video content.
predicates.evidence: An alternative for time-based media is provided that presents equivalent information for prerecorded audio-only content.

predicates.definition: Judge that the name, title, heading, or label accurately describes the topic or purpose of the element it identifies.
predicates.evidence: If non-text content is a control or accepts user input, then it has a name that describes its purpose.

predicates.definition: Inspect the implementation to confirm every interactive function can be operated through a keyboard interface.
predicates.evidence: All functionality of the content is operable through a keyboard interface

predicates.definition: Inspect the authentication flow to confirm an alternative method exists that does not rely on a cognitive function test.
predicates.evidence: Another authentication method that does not rely on a cognitive function test.

predicates.definition: Confirm an audio description is present and adequately conveys the meaningful visual content of the synchronized video.
predicates.evidence: An alternative for time-based media or audio description of the prerecorded video content is provided for synchronized media

predicates.definition: Confirm synchronized captions are present and accurately cover all prerecorded audio content.
predicates.evidence: Captions are provided for all prerecorded audio content in synchronized media

predicates.definition: Inspect the implementation to confirm the change occurs only as a result of an explicit user action.
predicates.evidence: unless a change is initiated by the user

predicates.definition: Inspect the authentication steps to confirm none require a cognitive function test such as recalling a password or solving a puzzle.
predicates.evidence: A cognitive function test (such as remembering a password or solving a puzzle) is not required for any step in an authentication process

predicates.definition: Inspect the submission flow to confirm the user is given an opportunity to correct detected input errors.
predicates.evidence: the user is provided an opportunity to correct them

predicates.definition: Inspect the implementation to confirm user-entered data is validated for input errors.
predicates.evidence: CheckedData entered by the user is checked for input errors

predicates.definition: Judge that the link's purpose can be understood from its link text alone or together with its programmatically associated context.
predicates.evidence: The purpose of each link can be determined from the link text alone

predicates.definition: Inspect the authentication flow to confirm a mechanism exists to help the user complete the cognitive function test.
predicates.evidence: A mechanism is available to assist the user in completing the cognitive function test.

predicates.definition: Inspect the implementation to confirm keyboard operation does not depend on specific timings for individual keystrokes.
predicates.evidence: without requiring specific timings for individual keystrokes

predicates.definition: Inspect the submission flow to confirm a mechanism lets the user review, confirm, and correct information before finalizing.
predicates.evidence: ConfirmedA mechanism is available for reviewing, confirming, and correcting information before finalizing the submission

predicates.definition: Inspect the submission flow to confirm a completed submission can be reversed.
predicates.evidence: ReversibleSubmissions are reversible

predicates.definition: Judge whether the particular visual presentation of the text is essential to the information being conveyed.
predicates.evidence: A particular presentation of text is essential to the information being conveyed

predicates.definition: Inspect the re-authentication flow to confirm the user can resume the activity without loss of previously entered data.
predicates.evidence: the user can continue the activity without loss of data after re-authenticating

predicates.definition: Inspect the implementation to confirm a mechanism dismisses the additional content without requiring the pointer or keyboard focus to move.
predicates.evidence: Dismissible A mechanism is available to dismiss the additional content without moving pointer hover or keyboard focus

predicates.definition: Inspect/test that the pointer can be moved onto hover-triggered additional content without it disappearing.
predicates.evidence: Hoverable If pointer hover can trigger the additional content, then the pointer can be moved over the additional content without the additional content disappearing

predicates.definition: Inspect/test that the additional content stays visible until the trigger is removed, the user dismisses it, or its information becomes invalid.
predicates.evidence: Persistent The additional content remains visible until the hover or focus trigger is removed, the user dismisses it, or its information is no longer valid

predicates.definition: Inspect the implementation to confirm a mechanism lets the user pause, stop, hide, or control the frequency of the auto-updating content.
predicates.evidence: there is a mechanism for the user to pause, stop, or hide it or to control the frequency of the update

predicates.definition: Judge whether the auto-updating is part of an activity for which it is essential.
predicates.evidence: unless the auto-updating is part of an activity where it is essential

predicates.definition: Measure the audio to confirm background sounds are at least 20 decibels below the foreground speech.
predicates.evidence: 20 dB The background sounds are at least 20 decibels lower than the foreground speech content

predicates.definition: Inspect the implementation to confirm a mechanism exists to turn the background sounds off.
predicates.evidence: Turn Off The background sounds can be turned off

predicates.definition: Check the markup confirms the decorative non-text content is implemented so assistive technology ignores it (e.g., empty alt, role=presentation, or aria-hidden).
predicates.evidence: it is implemented in a way that it can be ignored by assistive technology

predicates.definition: Inspect the implementation to confirm alternative CAPTCHA forms using different sensory modalities are provided.
predicates.evidence: alternative forms of CAPTCHA using output modes for different types of sensory perception are provided to accommodate different disabilities

predicates.definition: Judge that the CAPTCHA's text alternative identifies and describes its purpose.
predicates.evidence: text alternatives that identify and describe the purpose of the non-text content are provided

predicates.definition: Inspect the implementation to confirm changes to name, role, or value are exposed as notifications to user agents and assistive technologies.
predicates.evidence: notification of changes to these items is available to user agents, including assistive technologies

predicates.definition: Inspect the implementation to confirm changes of context occur only in response to an explicit user request.
predicates.evidence: Changes of context are initiated only by user request

predicates.definition: Inspect the event handling to confirm the function completes on the pointer up-event rather than the down-event.
predicates.evidence: Abort or Undo Completion of the function is on the up-event

predicates.definition: Computationally verify that no content on the page flashes more than three times within any one-second period.
predicates.evidence: Web pages do not contain anything that flashes more than three times in any one second period.

predicates.definition: Inspect the implementation to confirm context-sensitive help is provided for the content requiring user input.
predicates.evidence: Context-sensitive help is available

predicates.definition: Judge that the programmatically determined sequence of content matches the correct meaningful reading order.
predicates.evidence: a correct reading sequence can be programmatically determined

predicates.definition: Inspect the implementation to confirm correction suggestions are presented to the user when an input error is detected and suggestions are known.
predicates.evidence: the suggestions are provided to the user

predicates.definition: Inspect the implementation to confirm user-entered data is preserved for more than 20 hours of inactivity.
predicates.evidence: unless the data is preserved for more than 20 hours when the user does not take any actions

predicates.definition: Check that the page declares a valid default human language (e.g. a lang attribute on the html element).
predicates.evidence: The default human language of each web page can be programmatically determined

predicates.definition: Inspect the event handling to confirm no part of the function is executed on the pointer down-event.
predicates.evidence: No Down-Event The down-event of the pointer is not used to execute any part of the function

predicates.definition: Inspect the page to confirm an equivalent control achieving the same function exists and measures at least 44 by 44 CSS pixels.
predicates.evidence: Equivalent The target is available through an equivalent link or control on the same page that is at least 44 by 44 CSS pixels

predicates.definition: Inspect the implementation to confirm the specific item in error is identified to the user when an input error is detected.
predicates.evidence: the item that is in error is identified

predicates.definition: Inspect the implementation to confirm the detected error is conveyed to the user as text rather than by non-text means alone.
predicates.evidence: the error is described to the user in text

predicates.definition: Judge that extended audio description is provided and adequately conveys the essential visual information for the prerecorded video.
predicates.evidence: extended audio description is provided for all prerecorded video content in synchronized media

predicates.definition: Computationally verify that any flashing content stays below the general flash and red flash thresholds.
predicates.evidence: or the flash is below the general flash and red flash thresholds

predicates.definition: Inspect the implementation to confirm keyboard focus can be moved away from the component using only the keyboard (no keyboard trap).
predicates.evidence: then focus can be moved away from that component using only a keyboard interface

predicates.definition: Inspect the focus event handling to confirm receiving focus does not initiate a change of context.
predicates.evidence: When any user interface component receives focus, it does not initiate a change of context

predicates.definition: Measure that the focus indicator area is at least as large as a 2 CSS pixel thick perimeter of the component.
predicates.evidence: is at least as large as the area of a 2 CSS pixel thick perimeter of the unfocused component or sub-component

predicates.definition: Measure that the contrast ratio of the changed pixels between focused and unfocused states is at least 3:1.
predicates.evidence: has a contrast ratio of at least 3:1 between the same pixels in the focused and unfocused states

predicates.definition: Judge that the order in which focusable components receive focus preserves the meaning of the content.
predicates.evidence: focusable components receive focus in an order that preserves meaning and operability

predicates.definition: Inspect the focus order to confirm focusable components can still be operated in that sequence.
predicates.evidence: focusable components receive focus in an order that preserves meaning and operability

predicates.definition: Inspect the implementation to confirm no part of a component is hidden by author-created content when it receives keyboard focus.
predicates.evidence: When a user interface component receives keyboard focus, no part of the component is hidden by author-created content.

predicates.definition: Inspect the implementation to confirm a component is not entirely hidden by author-created content when it receives keyboard focus.
predicates.evidence: When a user interface component receives keyboard focus, the component is not entirely hidden due to author-created content.

predicates.definition: Inspect the implementation to confirm a mechanism lets the user select foreground and background colors for text.
predicates.evidence: Foreground and background colors can be selected by the user

predicates.definition: Inspect the page to confirm the same function can be achieved through a different control that meets the criterion.
predicates.evidence: The function can be achieved through a different control on the same page that meets this criterion

predicates.definition: Inspect the implementation to confirm any dragging-based functionality can also be operated by a single pointer without dragging.
predicates.evidence: All functionality that uses a dragging movement for operation can be achieved by a single pointer without dragging

predicates.definition: Judge which graphical parts are required to understand the content and verify they have at least 3:1 contrast against adjacent colors.
predicates.evidence: Graphical Objects Parts of graphics required to understand the content ... have a contrast ratio of at least 3:1 against adjacent color(s)

predicates.definition: Judge that any image of text serves only pure decoration and conveys no information to the user.
predicates.evidence: Images of text are only used for pure decoration

predicates.definition: Inspect the implementation to confirm the image of text can be visually customized to the user's requirements.
predicates.evidence: The image of text can be visually customized to the user's requirements

predicates.definition: Inspect the implementation to confirm the user is warned of the inactivity duration that could cause data loss.
predicates.evidence: Users are warned of the duration of any user inactivity that could cause data loss

predicates.definition: Judge that the information, structure, and relationships conveyed visually are equivalently available in text.
predicates.evidence: Information, structure, and relationships conveyed through presentation can be programmatically determined or are available in text.

predicates.definition: Inspect the markup to confirm the information, structure, and relationships conveyed through presentation are programmatically determinable via semantic markup.
predicates.evidence: Information, structure, and relationships conveyed through presentation can be programmatically determined

predicates.definition: Information the user previously entered (or that the system has about them) is auto-populated, so it need not be re-entered in the same process.
predicates.evidence: is either: auto-populated

predicates.definition: Confirm that information the user previously entered in the same process is offered for selection (or auto-populated) rather than requiring manual re-entry.
predicates.evidence: available for the user to select

predicates.definition: Judge that any information, action, or distinction signaled by color is also conveyed through a non-color visual means such as text, shape, or pattern.
predicates.evidence: Color is not used as the only visual means of conveying information, indicating an action, prompting a response, or distinguishing a visual element.

predicates.definition: Judge that informational text is rendered as real text rather than as an image of text, except where the presentation is customizable or essential.
predicates.evidence: text is used to convey information rather than images of text

predicates.definition: Inspect the content and code to confirm it does not block or restrict input modalities (keyboard, mouse, touch, voice) available on the platform.
predicates.evidence: Web content does not restrict use of input modalities available on a platform

predicates.definition: Check that input fields collecting information about the user expose a valid programmatic purpose, such as a correct autocomplete token.
predicates.evidence: The purpose of each input field collecting information about the user can be programmatically determined

predicates.definition: Inspect the form or control to confirm instructions are present wherever content requires user input.
predicates.evidence: Labels or instructions are provided when content requires user input

predicates.definition: Judge that instructions for understanding and operating content do not rely solely on sensory characteristics such as shape, color, size, visual location, orientation, or sound.
predicates.evidence: Instructions provided for understanding and operating content do not rely solely on sensory characteristics of components such as shape, color, size, visual location, orientation, or sound.

predicates.definition: Inspect the implementation to confirm motion animation triggered by interaction can be disabled, for example by honoring prefers-reduced-motion or a user control.
predicates.evidence: Motion animation triggered by interaction can be disabled, unless the animation is essential to the functionality or the information being conveyed.

predicates.definition: Inspect the implementation to confirm non-emergency interruptions can be postponed by the user.
predicates.evidence: Interruptions can be postponed or suppressed by the user, except interruptions involving an emergency

predicates.definition: Inspect the implementation to confirm non-emergency interruptions can be suppressed by the user.
predicates.evidence: Interruptions can be postponed or suppressed by the user, except interruptions involving an emergency

predicates.definition: Inspect the component's styles to confirm a visible keyboard focus indicator is present in at least one mode of operation.
predicates.evidence: Any keyboard operable user interface has a mode of operation where the keyboard focus indicator is visible.

predicates.definition: Check that controls requiring user input have an associated programmatic label.
predicates.evidence: Labels or instructions are provided when content requires user input

predicates.definition: Compute that large-scale text and images of large-scale text have a contrast ratio of at least 3:1 against the background.
predicates.evidence: Large-scale text and images of large-scale text have a contrast ratio of at least 3:1

predicates.definition: Compute that large-scale text and images of large-scale text have a contrast ratio of at least 4.5:1 against the background.
predicates.evidence: Large-scale text and images of large-scale text have a contrast ratio of at least 4.5:1

predicates.definition: Compute that line spacing is at least 1.5 times within paragraphs and paragraph spacing is at least 1.5 times the line spacing.
predicates.evidence: Line spacing (leading) is at least space-and-a-half within paragraphs, and paragraph spacing is at least 1.5 times larger than the line spacing

predicates.definition: Inspect the page for a mechanism such as a breadcrumb, current-page indicator, or sitemap that conveys the user's location within the set of pages.
predicates.evidence: Information about the user's location within a set of web pages is available.

predicates.definition: Inspect the pointer-operated control to confirm a mechanism exists to abort the function before completion.
predicates.evidence: a mechanism is available to abort the function before completion

predicates.definition: Inspect the content for a mechanism such as a glossary, inline definition, or link that identifies definitions of words or phrases used in an unusual or restricted way.
predicates.evidence: A mechanism is available for identifying specific definitions of words or phrases used in an unusual or restricted way, including idioms and jargon

predicates.definition: Inspect the content for a mechanism that identifies the expanded form or meaning of abbreviations.
predicates.evidence: A mechanism for identifying the expanded form or meaning of abbreviations is available

predicates.definition: Inspect the content for a mechanism that identifies the pronunciation of words whose meaning is ambiguous without it.
predicates.evidence: A mechanism is available for identifying specific pronunciation of words where meaning of the words, in context, is ambiguous without knowing the pronunciation

predicates.definition: Inspect the pointer-operated control to confirm a mechanism exists to undo the function after completion.
predicates.evidence: to undo the function after completion

predicates.definition: Inspect the page for a mechanism such as a skip link, landmarks, or headings that bypasses blocks of content repeated across pages.
predicates.evidence: A mechanism is available to bypass blocks of content that are repeated on multiple web pages.

predicates.definition: Inspect the audio control to confirm volume can be adjusted independently from the overall system volume.
predicates.evidence: a mechanism is available to control audio volume independently from the overall system volume level

predicates.definition: Inspect auto-playing audio that lasts more than three seconds to confirm a mechanism is available to pause or stop it.
predicates.evidence: either a mechanism is available to pause or stop the audio

predicates.definition: Inspect the implementation to confirm a mechanism is available to turn off automatically initiated changes of context.
predicates.evidence: or a mechanism is available to turn off such changes

predicates.definition: Inspect the device-motion-operated functionality to confirm response to motion can be disabled to prevent accidental actuation.
predicates.evidence: responding to the motion can be disabled to prevent accidental actuation

predicates.definition: Inspect moving, blinking, or scrolling content that lasts more than five seconds to confirm a mechanism to pause, stop, or hide it is available.
predicates.evidence: there is a mechanism for the user to pause, stop, or hide it

predicates.definition: Judge whether the moving, blinking, or scrolling is part of an activity where it is essential, qualifying for the exception.
predicates.evidence: unless the movement, blinking, or scrolling is part of an activity where it is essential

predicates.definition: Inspect the site to confirm more than one way (e.g. navigation menu, search, sitemap) is available to locate a page within the set of pages.
predicates.evidence: More than one way is available to locate a web page within a set of web pages

predicates.definition: Check that a control's accessible name contains the text presented to it visually as its label.
predicates.evidence: the name contains the text that is presented visually

predicates.definition: Confirm the component exposes an accessible name to the accessibility API that can be programmatically read.
predicates.evidence: the name and role can be programmatically determined

predicates.definition: Listen to the audio and confirm it contains no background sounds behind the foreground speech.
predicates.evidence: No Background The audio does not contain background sounds

predicates.definition: Resize text up to 200 percent and confirm no content is clipped and all functionality still works.
predicates.evidence: up to 200 percent without loss of content or functionality

predicates.definition: Apply the WCAG text-spacing overrides (line, paragraph, letter, word spacing) and confirm no content or functionality is lost.
predicates.evidence: no loss of content or functionality occurs by setting all of the following and by changing no other style property: Line height (line spacing) to at least 1.5 times the font size; Spacing following paragraphs to at least 2 times the font size; Letter spacing (tracking) to at least 0.12 times the font size; Word spacing to at least 0.16 times the font size

predicates.definition: Reflow the content to a narrow viewport and confirm no information or functionality is lost.
predicates.evidence: Content can be presented without loss of information or functionality

predicates.definition: Analyze the content and confirm nothing flashes more than three times in any one-second period.
predicates.evidence: Web pages do not contain anything that flashes more than three times in any one second period

predicates.definition: Measure scroll dimensions at 320 CSS pixels wide and confirm content does not require scrolling in two dimensions.
predicates.evidence: without requiring scrolling in two dimensions for: Vertical scrolling content at a width equivalent to 320 CSS pixels; Horizontal scrolling content at a height equivalent to 256 CSS pixels

predicates.definition: Confirm that when exiting requires non-standard keys, the markup or content advises the user of the method to move focus away.
predicates.evidence: the user is advised of the method for moving focus away

predicates.definition: Confirm the device-motion function can also be operated through conventional user interface controls.
predicates.evidence: can also be operated by user interface components

predicates.definition: Confirm the function can be operated with a single pointer without requiring a path-based or multipoint gesture.
predicates.evidence: can be operated with a single pointer without a path-based gesture

predicates.definition: Judge whether the specific presentation of the target is essential or legally required for the information conveyed.
predicates.evidence: A particular presentation of the target is essential or is legally required for the information being conveyed

predicates.definition: Confirm passages or phrases in another language carry a valid lang attribute so their language is programmatically determinable.
predicates.evidence: The human language of each passage or phrase in the content can be programmatically determined

predicates.definition: Confirm status content is marked up (e.g. live region) so assistive technologies can present it without it receiving focus.
predicates.evidence: they can be presented to the user by assistive technologies without receiving focus

predicates.definition: Judge whether providing error-correction suggestions would jeopardize the security or purpose of the content.
predicates.evidence: unless it would jeopardize the security or purpose of the content

predicates.definition: Judge whether the purpose of user interface components, icons, and regions is conveyed through semantics that capture that meaning.
predicates.evidence: the purpose of user interface components, icons, and regions can be programmatically determined

predicates.definition: Compare help mechanisms across the page set and confirm they appear in the same order relative to other page content.
predicates.evidence: those mechanisms are repeated on multiple web pages within a set of web pages, they occur in the same order relative to other page content

predicates.definition: Compare repeated navigation across the page set and confirm it occurs in the same relative order each time.
predicates.evidence: Navigational mechanisms that are repeated on multiple web pages within a set of web pages occur in the same relative order each time they are repeated

predicates.definition: Confirm the component exposes a role to the accessibility API that can be programmatically read.
predicates.evidence: the name and role can be programmatically determined

predicates.definition: Judge whether components with the same functionality across the page set are labeled and identified consistently.
predicates.evidence: Components that have the same functionality within a set of web pages are identified consistently

predicates.definition: Judge whether section headings are used to appropriately organize the page's content.
predicates.evidence: Section headings are used to organize the content.

predicates.definition: Confirm changing the component's setting does not automatically trigger a change of context.
predicates.evidence: Changing the setting of any user interface component does not automatically cause a change of context

predicates.definition: Confirm the keyboard shortcut is active only when its associated component has focus.
predicates.evidence: Active only on focus The keyboard shortcut for a user interface component is only active when that component has focus

predicates.definition: Confirm a mechanism exists to remap the single-character shortcut to include one or more non-printable keys.
predicates.evidence: Remap A mechanism is available to remap the shortcut to include one or more non-printable keyboard keys (e.g., Ctrl, Alt)

predicates.definition: Confirm a mechanism is available to turn the single-character keyboard shortcut off.
predicates.evidence: Turn off A mechanism is available to turn the shortcut off

predicates.definition: Confirm sign language interpretation is provided for the prerecorded audio in the synchronized media.
predicates.evidence: Sign language interpretation is provided for all prerecorded audio content in synchronized media.

predicates.definition: Judge whether a version is available that does not require reading ability beyond the lower secondary education level.
predicates.evidence: a version that does not require reading ability more advanced than the lower secondary education level, is available

predicates.definition: Confirm focus can be moved away from the component using only standard exit methods such as Tab or arrow keys.
predicates.evidence: if it requires more than unmodified arrow or tab keys or other standard exit methods

predicates.definition: Confirm status messages are exposed through appropriate role or properties so they can be programmatically determined without focus.
predicates.evidence: status messages can be programmatically determined through role or properties

predicates.definition: Judge whether supplemental content is available that aids comprehension of text requiring advanced reading ability.
predicates.evidence: supplemental content, or a version that does not require reading ability more advanced than the lower secondary education level, is available

predicates.definition: Measure the pointer target and confirm it is at least 44 by 44 CSS pixels.
predicates.evidence: The size of the target for pointer inputs is at least 44 by 44 CSS pixels

predicates.definition: Confirm the target sits within a sentence or its size is otherwise constrained by the line-height of surrounding non-target text.
predicates.evidence: The target is in a sentence or its size is otherwise constrained by the line-height of non-target text

predicates.definition: Compute the pointer target's bounding box and confirm it is at least 24 by 24 CSS pixels.
predicates.evidence: The size of the target for pointer inputs is at least 24 by 24 CSS pixels

predicates.definition: Confirm the target's size is the user-agent default and is not modified by author styling.
predicates.evidence: The size of the target is determined by the user agent and is not modified by the author

predicates.definition: Confirm the authentication cognitive-function test only requires the user to recognize objects.
predicates.evidence: The cognitive function test is to recognize objects.

predicates.definition: Confirm the authentication cognitive-function test only requires identifying non-text content the user previously provided to the site.
predicates.evidence: The cognitive function test is to identify non-text content the user provided to the website.

predicates.definition: Confirm every non-text content element has an associated text alternative (e.g., alt, aria-label, or accessible name).
predicates.evidence: All non-text content that is presented to the user has a text alternative that serves the equivalent purpose

predicates.definition: Judge that the text alternative at least descriptively identifies the non-text content.
predicates.evidence: text alternatives at least provide descriptive identification of the non-text content

predicates.definition: Judge that the text alternative conveys the same purpose and information as the non-text content it replaces.
predicates.evidence: All non-text content that is presented to the user has a text alternative that serves the equivalent purpose

predicates.definition: Compute the contrast ratio between the text and its background and confirm it is at least 4.5:1.
predicates.evidence: The visual presentation of text and images of text has a contrast ratio of at least 4.5:1

predicates.definition: Compute the contrast ratio between the text and its background and confirm it is at least 7:1.
predicates.evidence: The visual presentation of text and images of text has a contrast ratio of at least 7:1

predicates.definition: Confirm the text block is not justified to both margins (computed text-align is not 'justify').
predicates.evidence: Text is not justified (aligned to both the left and the right margins)

predicates.definition: Confirm text can be enlarged to 200% without assistive technology and without requiring horizontal scrolling to read a line on a full-screen window.
predicates.evidence: Text can be resized without assistive technology up to 200 percent in a way that does not require the user to scroll horizontally to read a line of text on a full-screen window

predicates.definition: Confirm text can be resized up to 200% without assistive technology and without loss of content or functionality.
predicates.evidence: text can be resized without assistive technology up to 200 percent

predicates.definition: Confirm a mechanism lets the user adjust the time limit before encountering it over a range at least ten times the default setting.
predicates.evidence: Adjust The user is allowed to adjust the time limit before encountering it over a wide range that is at least ten times the length of the default setting

predicates.definition: Confirm the user is warned before the time limit expires and can extend it with a simple action giving at least 20 seconds.
predicates.evidence: Extend The user is warned before time expires and given at least 20 seconds to extend the time limit with a simple action

predicates.definition: Confirm a mechanism lets the user turn off the time limit before encountering it.
predicates.evidence: Turn off The user is allowed to turn off the time limit before encountering it

predicates.definition: Judge that the time limit is essential and that extending it would invalidate the activity.
predicates.evidence: Essential Exception The time limit is essential and extending it would invalidate the activity

predicates.definition: Confirm the configured time limit is longer than 20 hours.
predicates.evidence: 20 Hour Exception The time limit is longer than 20 hours

predicates.definition: Judge that the time limit is a required part of a real-time event with no possible alternative.
predicates.evidence: Real-time Exception The time limit is a required part of a real-time event (for example, an auction), and no alternative to the time limit is possible

predicates.definition: Judge that timing is not an essential part of the activity, excluding non-interactive synchronized media and real-time events.
predicates.evidence: Timing is not an essential part of the event or activity presented by the content, except for non-interactive synchronized media and real-time events

predicates.definition: Confirm the page has a non-empty title element.
predicates.evidence: Web pages have titles

predicates.definition: Compute the contrast of visual information needed to identify UI components and states and confirm it is at least 3:1 against adjacent colors.
predicates.evidence: User Interface Components Visual information required to identify user interface components and states ... have a contrast ratio of at least 3:1 against adjacent color(s)

predicates.definition: Confirm that 24 CSS pixel diameter circles centered on undersized targets do not intersect another target or another such circle.
predicates.evidence: Undersized targets (those less than 24 by 24 CSS pixels) are positioned so that if a 24 CSS pixel diameter circle is centered on the bounding box of each, the circles do not intersect another target or the circle for another undersized target

predicates.definition: Confirm the pointer up-event reverses any outcome produced by the preceding down-event.
predicates.evidence: Up Reversal The up-event reverses any outcome of the preceding down-event

predicates.definition: Confirm the user is informed of the component's context-changing behavior before they use it.
predicates.evidence: unless the user has been advised of the behavior before using the component

predicates.definition: Confirm states, properties, and values the user can set are also programmatically settable.
predicates.evidence: states, properties, and values that can be set by the user can be programmatically set

predicates.definition: Confirm the content does not lock its view and operation to a single display orientation unless a specific orientation is essential.
predicates.evidence: Content does not restrict its view and operation to a single display orientation, such as portrait or landscape, unless a specific display orientation is essential.

predicates.definition: Confirm blocks of text are no wider than 80 characters per line (40 for CJK).
predicates.evidence: Width is no more than 80 characters or glyphs (40 if CJK)

criteria.verification_expression: (text-alternative-present AND text-alternative-serves-equivalent-purpose) OR describes-topic-or-purpose OR text-alternative-provides-descriptive-identification OR (captcha-text-alternative-describes-purpose AND captcha-alternative-sensory-forms-provided) OR can-be-ignored-by-assistive-technology

foreground-and-background-colors-user-selectable AND width-no-more-than-80-characters AND text-not-justified AND line-and-paragraph-spacing-sufficient AND text-resizable-to-200-percent-without-horizontal-scrolling

time-limit-can-be-turned-off OR time-limit-can-be-adjusted OR time-limit-can-be-extended OR time-limit-required-for-real-time-event OR time-limit-essential-to-activity OR time-limit-longer-than-twenty-hours

down-event-not-used-to-execute-function OR (completion-on-up-event AND (mechanism-available-to-abort-before-completion OR mechanism-available-to-undo-after-completion)) OR up-event-reverses-down-event-outcome
